description.task: Implement DFS-based topological sorting for a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG). After fully exploring each vertex, push it onto a stack. Pop the stack for topological order. Use the graph: 5→2, 5→0, 4→0, 4→1, 2→3, 3→1.
instructions: Valid topological order: vertices 4 and 5 must appear before 0, 1, 2, 3
One valid output: 5 4 2 3 1 0 (but exact order depends on DFS traversal sequence)
Topological sort only works on DAGs — cycles would cause infinite recursion

description.task: Implement Kosaraju's algorithm to find Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) in a directed graph. Step 1: DFS on original graph, push finish order to stack. Step 2: DFS on transposed graph in stack order.
instructions: In findSCCs pass 2: dfs2(v,vis2,comp); Collections.sort(comp); System.out.println("SCC: "+comp);
Pass 1 uses original adjacency; Pass 2 uses reversed adjacency
Kosaraju runs in O(V+E) time — two DFS passes

description.task: Detect cycles in a directed graph using DFS with a recursion stack. Maintain a 'recStack' boolean array to track vertices in the current DFS path. A back edge (to a node in recStack) indicates a cycle.
instructions: recStack tracks the current DFS path; if we revisit a node in the current path, it's a cycle
Graph1 (0→1→2→3, no back edges) = no cycle → false
Must set recStack[v]=false when backtracking to avoid false positives from other DFS paths
description.expectedOutput:
Graph1 has cycle: false
Graph2 has cycle: true
